title: A City's Ban on Electronic Protests
article_text: The city council made a command to reject the proposed electronic protest in the square. A large circle of people gathered to show their sadness, but the latter group was unable to change the decision. The index of public opinion was weak, and many citizens felt sick about the ban. A helpful journalist wrote a column about the event, which left a strong impression on readers. The police used a plate to steal the protesters' signs, which was a joke to some people. The sky was gray, and the atmosphere was sad as people walked in a circle. The city frequently used electronic devices to monitor the protest, which was not attractive to the public. The protestors had faith that their actions would compete with the city's power. The reduction in free speech made many people feel sick and unable to protest. The latter part of the day was quiet, and people went home with a sad impression.

image_prompts: A city council meeting room with officials making a command to reject an electronic protest sign.
A large circle of sad people in a square, with a smaller group (the latter) looking unable to change anything.
A chart showing a weak index of public opinion, with citizens looking sick near a ban sign.
A journalist writing a helpful column at a desk, with a strong impression symbol above readers.
A police officer using a plate to steal signs from protesters, with people laughing as if it is a joke.
A gray sky over a sad crowd walking in a circle in a square.
A city with electronic devices monitoring a protest, with unattractive gray tones.
Protestors with faith holding signs, competing with a large city government building.
A graph showing a reduction in free speech, with sick and unable people.
A quiet evening scene with people walking home, looking sad, with the latter part of the day in twilight.
